docs.title: 2026 Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation page of the 2026 IEEE Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies!

This is a joint effort between the IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Society, the Future Directions Initiative on Telepresence, and the Robotics and Automation Society.

docs.title: Support & Feedback
docs.text: Welcome to the Vicarios Support Hub. Whether you've found a bug in a robot controller or need help with a plugin integration, please use the channels below to reach our development team.

docs.title: 🛠 Open a GitHub Issue
docs.text:
Select the repository that corresponds to the project you are working with. Using these links will automatically apply the structured issue template.

  • Robots

  • Robot Importer

  • Middleware & Comms

docs.title: 📝 Submission Guidelines
docs.text:
To help our engineers resolve your issue as quickly as possible, please include the following in your report:

Required Information

  • Environment: Operating System (e.g., Ubuntu 22.04, Windows 11).
  • Versions: Software version or Git commit hash.
  • Logs: Paste relevant error logs using code blocks ( ` ` ` ).
  • Reproduction: A clear list of steps to recreate the bug.

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• Documentation Hub — Detailed setup and API guides.
  • Technical Wiki — Community-driven tutorials and troubleshooting.
  • Release Notes — Check if your issue was fixed in a recent update.

docs.title: Software Repositories
docs.text: The Vicarios ecosystem consists of several interconnected repositories designed for high-performance robotics, real-time middleware, and immersive simulation.

docs.title: Vicarios Robots
docs.text:
Hardware Control & Configuration The central repository for robot-specific drivers and hardware abstraction layers. It provides the necessary interface logic to connect physical robotic platforms with high-level control software.

  • Integration: Works in tandem with the Robot Importer for digital twin synchronization.
  • Support: Open structured issues for hardware-specific bugs here.

docs.title: EasyDDS
docs.text:
Middleware & Communication A streamlined, lightweight C++ interface for Data Distribution Service (DDS) messaging. It abstracts the complexity of standard DDS, making it easier to implement low-latency communication in ROS 2 environments.

  • Performance: Optimized for real-time telemetry and command streaming.
  • Support: Open structured issues for middleware bugs here.

docs.title: Robot Importer (Unreal)
docs.text:
Simulation & Visualization A specialized plugin for Unreal Engine 5 that facilitates the import and simulation of complex robotic models (URDF/MJCF). It is essential for projects involving immersive teleoperation and high-fidelity rendering.

  • Features: Supports foveated rendering and real-time unicasting.
  • Support: Open structured issues for simulation bugs here.

docs.title: 2026 Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation page of the 2026 IEEE Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies!

This is a joint effort between the IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Society, the Future Directions Initiative on Telepresence, and the Robotics and Automation Society.

docs.title: 2026 Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies
docs.text:
Academic References

  • IEEE Telepresence Initiative White Paper Chasing a Dream: The Quest for Minsky’s Concept of Telepresence. Read on Telepresence Site
  • ROBOT TELEOPERATIVO (2024): Collaborative Cybernetic Systems for Immersive Remote Teleoperation. Read on IEEE Xplore
  • FoReCast (2022): Real-time Foveated Rendering and Unicasting for Immersive Remote Telepresence. Read on Eurographics
